history.text: The territory became part of the West Indies Federation, shaping its later political and regional identity.
Barbados became independent as a Commonwealth realm, keeping the monarch as head of state at first.
Barbados was admitted to the United Nations, confirming broad international recognition.
Barbados joined CARICOM at the Treaty of Chaguaramas, tying the country more closely to that regional or international organisation.
A republic was declared, changing Barbados' political system and state identity.

worthSeeing.title: Rum Heritage:
worthSeeing.text: Birthplace of Caribbean rum, with Mount Gay distillery operating since 1703.

worthSeeing.title: Bridgetown UNESCO:
worthSeeing.text: The historic capital and its garrison, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

worthSeeing.title: East Coast Surf:
worthSeeing.text: Soup Bowl at Bathsheba is a world-famous reef break for surfers.
